4370	\subsection{Iter-189 analogist-licensed unbundle pins}
4371	\label{sec:quot_iter189_unbundle}
4372	
4373	The iter-189 \texttt{mathlib-analogist lane-f-isbasechange} verdict
4374	(see \texttt{analogies/lane-f-isbasechange.md}) returned verdict (A)
4375	\emph{STRUCTURAL OK} on the project's use of
4376	\texttt{IsBaseChange.of\_equiv}. The corrective was to \emph{unbundle}
4377	the iter-188 \(\Sigma\)-pair helper's three internal Mathlib gaps into
4378	separately named typed-sorry pins so that each can be targeted
4379	individually (~30-50 LOC apiece) in subsequent iters. Iter-189 Lane F
4380	prover landed the unbundle. The two new pins are recorded below
4381	(parallel to the existing \cref{lem:pullback_tildeIso} pin, which
4382	remains Step 2 of the 3-step chain).
4383	
4384	\begin{lemma}
4385	
4386	\leanok
4387	[Quasi-coherent module on an affine open is \texttt{tilde} of its sections (Stacks 01I8)]
4388	  \label{lem:tildeIso_of_isQuasicoherent_isAffineOpen}
4389	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.tildeIso_of_isQuasicoherent_isAffineOpen}
4390	  \uses{def:quot_canonical_basechange_map}
4391	  \textit{Source: Stacks~\href{https://stacks.math.columbia.edu/tag/01I8}{01I8}
4392	  (a quasi-coherent module on an affine scheme is \(\widetilde M\) for
4393	  some module).}
4394	  Let \(X\) be a scheme, \(\mathcal N\) a quasi-coherent
4395	  \(\mathcal O_X\)-module, and \(V \subset X\) an affine open. Then the
4396	  pullback of \(\mathcal N\) along \texttt{hV.fromSpec} is canonically
4397	  isomorphic to \texttt{tilde} of its sections:
4398	  \[
4399	    (\mathtt{Scheme.Modules.pullback}\,\mathtt{hV.fromSpec}).\mathrm{obj}\,
4400	      \mathcal N
4401	    \;\cong\;
4402	    \widetilde{\,\Gamma(\mathcal N, V)\,}
4403	    \quad\text{(on \(\mathrm{Spec}\,\Gamma(X, V)\)).}
4404	  \]
4405	  Mathlib \texttt{b80f227} ships the analog for \emph{free}
4406	  quasi-coherent modules (\texttt{isIso\_fromTildeΓ\_of\_presentation}
4407	  partial), but the general statement
4408	  ``\([N.IsQuasicoherent] \Rightarrow N|_V \cong \widetilde{\Gamma(N,V)}\)
4409	  on an affine open \(V\)'' (Stacks 01I8 \emph{lemma-affine-qc-module-iff})
4410	  is unowned at the \texttt{Scheme.Modules} layer.
4411	\end{lemma}
4412	\begin{proof}
4413	  \leanok
4414	  \uses{def:quot_canonical_basechange_map, lem:modules_pullback_mathlib,
4415	    lem:modules_restrictFunctorIsoPullback_mathlib}
4416	  Write \(j = \mathtt{hV.fromSpec} : \Spec\Gamma(X,V) \to X\) (an open
4417	  immersion with image \(V\)) and \(G = j^{*}\mathcal N\). Pullback along an
4418	  open immersion preserves quasi-coherence
4419	  (Lean \texttt{isQuasicoherent\_pullback\_fromSpec}), so \(G\) is a
4420	  quasi-coherent module on the affine scheme \(\Spec\Gamma(X,V)\); by the
4421	  affine structure theorem (Stacks 01I8, Lean
4422	  \texttt{isIso\_fromTildeΓ\_of\_isQuasicoherent}) the tilde--\(\Gamma\)
4423	  counit \(\widetilde{\Gamma(G,\top)} \to G\) is an isomorphism.
4424	
4425	  The canonical base map \(b : \Gamma(\mathcal N, V) \to \Gamma(G, \top)\)
4426	  (the \(V\)-sections of the pullback--pushforward adjunction unit followed by
4427	  restriction along \(\top \le j^{-1}V\)) is bijective. Indeed the two
4428	  adjunctions \(\mathrm{restrict} \dashv j_{*}\) and \(j^{*} \dashv j_{*}\)
4429	  share the right adjoint, so their units are identified through the canonical
4430	  natural isomorphism \(\mathrm{restrict} \cong j^{*}\)
4431	  (\cref{lem:modules_restrictFunctorIsoPullback_mathlib}). Under this
4432	  identification, the \(V\)-component of the unit of the restriction
4433	  adjunction is the presheaf restriction of \(\mathcal N\) along the
4434	  \emph{equality} of opens \(j(j^{-1}V) = V\), hence bijective; the component
4435	  of the natural isomorphism is bijective; and the final restriction is along
4436	  the equality \(\top = j^{-1}V\). Thus \(b\) is a composite of three
4437	  bijections.
4438	
4439	  Since \(b\) is a bijective \(\Gamma(X,V)\)-linear map (the
4440	  \(\Gamma(X,V)\)-action on \(\Gamma(G,\top)\) being through the canonical
4441	  ring isomorphism \(\Gamma(X,V) \cong \Gamma(\Spec\Gamma(X,V),\top)\)),
4442	  it induces an isomorphism
4443	  \(\widetilde{b} : \widetilde{\Gamma(\mathcal N,V)} \cong
4444	  \widetilde{\Gamma(G,\top)}\). The required isomorphism is the inverse of
4445	  \(\widetilde{b}\) followed by the counit. The characterizing section
4446	  identity holds because \(\widetilde{(-)}\)-functoriality intertwines the
4447	  canonical maps \(M \to \Gamma(\widetilde M, U)\) (naturality of
4448	  \texttt{toOpen}), while the counit composed with \(\mathtt{toOpen}\) at
4449	  \(\top\) is the identity restriction.
4450	\end{proof}
4451	
4452	\begin{lemma}
4453	
4454	\leanok
4455	[Section-level transport for pullback along an affine open's
4456	\texttt{fromSpec}]
4457	  \label{lem:pullback_of_openImmersion_iso_restrict}
4458	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pullback_of_openImmersion_iso_restrict}
4459	  \uses{def:quot_canonical_basechange_map}
4460	  \textit{Source: Project-bespoke transport, formed from
4461	  Stacks~\href{https://stacks.math.columbia.edu/tag/01HH}{01HH} +
4462	  \texttt{tilde.isoTop}.}
4463	  Let \(Y\) be a scheme, \(\mathcal N\) an \(\mathcal O_Y\)-module, and
4464	  \(U \subset Y\) an affine open. Equip \(\Gamma((\mathrm{Spec}\,\Gamma(Y, U)),
4465	  \top)\) with the \(\Gamma(Y, U)\)-algebra structure inherited from
4466	  \texttt{Scheme.ΓSpecIso} (the canonical
4467	  \(\Gamma(\mathrm{Spec}\,R, \top) \cong R\) for any commutative ring \(R\)),
4468	  and equip
4469	  \(\Gamma((\text{pullback}\,\mathtt{hU.fromSpec}).\mathrm{obj}\,\mathcal N,
4470	  \top)\) with the \(\Gamma(Y, U)\)-module structure inherited via
4471	  \texttt{Module.compHom}. Then there is a canonical \(\Gamma(Y, U)\)-linear
4472	  isomorphism between this pullback-section module and the original section
4473	  module \(\Gamma(\mathcal N, U)\):
4474	  \[
4475	    \Gamma((\text{pullback}\,\mathtt{hU.fromSpec}).\mathrm{obj}\,\mathcal N,
4476	      \top)
4477	    \;\simeq_{\Gamma(Y, U)}\;
4478	    \Gamma(\mathcal N, U).
4479	  \]
4480	  The map identifies a section of the pulled-back sheaf at \(\top\) of
4481	  \(\mathrm{Spec}\,\Gamma(Y, U)\) with the corresponding section of
4482	  \(\mathcal N\) on the affine open \(U \subset Y\). This pin is the
4483	  iter-190+ prover target as Step 3 of the iter-189 \(\Sigma\)-pair
4484	  unbundle.
4485	\end{lemma}
4486	\begin{proof}
4487	  \leanok
4488	  \uses{def:quot_canonical_basechange_map,
4489	    lem:modules_restrictFunctorIsoPullback_mathlib}
4490	  The underlying additive equivalence is the composite of the
4491	  \(\top\)-section of the natural isomorphism
4492	  \(j^{*} \cong \mathrm{restrict}\,j\) for the open immersion
4493	  \(j = \mathtt{hU.fromSpec}\)
4494	  (\cref{lem:modules_restrictFunctorIsoPullback_mathlib}) with the
4495	  restriction of \(\mathcal N\) along the equality of opens
4496	  \(j(\top) = U\); its \(\Gamma(Y, U)\)-linearity reduces (through
4497	  \texttt{Hom.app\_smul} and semilinearity of section restriction) to
4498	  the ring identity
4499	  \(\iota^{-1} \circ (j^{\sharp}\top)^{-1} \circ
4500	    \mathrm{res}_{U = j(\top)} = \mathrm{id}\), which follows from
4501	  \texttt{fromSpec\_app\_self}.
4502	
4503	  The additional \(\Sigma\)-pair characterization (T12, 2026-07-03) —
4504	  the inverse of the equivalence is the canonical base map — is the
4505	  unit-compatibility of \texttt{Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq} for the
4506	  restriction adjunction versus the pullback--pushforward adjunction,
4507	  as spelled out in \cref{lem:baseMap_inv_step3_open_immersion}.
4508	\end{proof}

1936	    (t : Finset R) (hspan : Ideal.span (t : Set R) = ⊤)
1937	    (Hfr : ∀ U : (Spec R).Opens, (∃ s : R, U = PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen s) →

1939	      IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f)

1942	    (y : ToType ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.obj (.op (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f)))) :
1943	    ∃ (x : ToType ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.obj (.op ⊤))) (n : ℕ),
1944	      f ^ n • y = ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.map

theorem map_units_restrict_basicOpen {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (M : (Spec R).Modules) (f : R) :
    ∀ x : Submonoid.powers f, IsUnit (algebraMap R (Module.End R
      ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.obj
        (.op (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f)))) (x : R)) := by
  rintro ⟨x, n, rfl⟩
  rw [map_pow]
  exact (Scheme.Modules.isUnit_algebraMap_end_of_le_basicOpen (M := M) f le_rfl).pow n

/-- **Finite basic-open cover refining a quasi-coherent presentation cover.** Given a
sheaf of modules `M` on `Spec R` together with quasi-coherent data `q` (a — possibly
infinite — open cover `q.X : q.I → (Spec R).Opens` of `⊤` with a presentation of
`M.over (q.X i)` on each member), there is a *finite* family of elements `t : Finset R`
whose basic opens cover `Spec R` (`Ideal.span t = ⊤`), with each basic open `D(r)`
(`r ∈ t`) contained in some member `q.X i` of the presentation cover.

theorem exists_finite_basicOpen_cover_le_quasicoherentData {R : CommRingCat.{u}}
    (M : (Spec R).Modules) (q : M.QuasicoherentData) :
    ∃ t : Finset R, Ideal.span (t : Set R) = ⊤ ∧
      ∀ r ∈ t, ∃ i, (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen r : (Spec R).Opens) ≤ q.X i := by
  classical
  set G : Set R := {r | ∃ i, (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen r : (Spec R).Opens) ≤ q.X i} with hG
  have hspanG : Ideal.span G = ⊤ := by
    rw [← PrimeSpectrum.iSup_basicOpen_eq_top_iff']
    rw [eq_top_iff]
    intro x _
    simp only [TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_iSup]
    obtain ⟨U, f, hf, hxU⟩ := q.coversTop ⊤ x (by trivial)
    rw [Sieve.mem_ofObjects_iff] at hf
    obtain ⟨i, ⟨hUi⟩⟩ := hf
    have hxXi : x ∈ q.X i := (leOfHom hUi) hxU
    obtain ⟨V, ⟨r, rfl⟩, hxV, hVle⟩ :=
      (TopologicalSpace.Opens.isBasis_iff_nbhd.mp PrimeSpectrum.isBasis_basic_opens) hxXi

Project-local: Mathlib has no QC-pullback lemma. -/
theorem isQuasicoherent_pullback_of_isOpenImmersion {Y : Scheme.{u}} (g : Y ⟶ X)
    [IsOpenImmersion g] (M : X.Modules) [M.IsQuasicoherent] :
    ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj M).IsQuasicoherent := by
  obtain ⟨q⟩ : Nonempty M.QuasicoherentData :=
    SheafOfModules.IsQuasicoherent.nonempty_quasicoherentData
  set qs := q.shrink with hqs
  haveI : ∀ i, (((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj M).over (g ⁻¹ᵁ (qs.X i))).IsQuasicoherent :=
    fun i => isQuasicoherent_over_preimage g M qs i
  exact SheafOfModules.IsQuasicoherent.of_coversTop ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj M)
    (fun i => g ⁻¹ᵁ (qs.X i)) (coversTop_preimage g M qs)

/-- **(Piece A, target) Quasi-coherence is preserved under pullback along `fromSpec`.**
For `M` quasi-coherent on `X` and an affine open `U`, the pullback of `M` along the affine immersion
`hU.fromSpec : Spec Γ(X, U) ⟶ X` is quasi-coherent. The `g := hU.fromSpec` instance of
`isQuasicoherent_pullback_of_isOpenImmersion` (`fromSpec` is an open immersion). This is the
QC-pullback input the gap2 close feeds to gap1. Project-local. -/
theorem isQuasicoherent_pullback_fromSpec (M : X.Modules) [M.IsQuasicoherent]
    {U : X.Opens} (hU : IsAffineOpen U) :
    ((Scheme.Modules.pullback hU.fromSpec).obj M).IsQuasicoherent :=
  isQuasicoherent_pullback_of_isOpenImmersion hU.fromSpec M

/-- **gap2 keystone (`lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen`): basic-open section localization for
a quasi-coherent sheaf on an arbitrary scheme.** For `M` quasi-coherent on `X`, an affine open `U`,
and `f : Γ(X, U)`, the section restriction `Γ(M, U) → Γ(M, X.basicOpen f)` is
`IsLocalizedModule (powers f)` over `Γ(X, U)`. Assembles the QC-pullback (Piece A,
`isQuasicoherent_pullback_fromSpec`) → gap1 (`isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_isQuasicoherent`) → the eqToHom
bridge (Piece B, `isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_of_hP1`). Project-local: closes gap2. -/
theorem isLocalizedModule_basicOpen (M : X.Modules) [M.IsQuasicoherent]
    {U : X.Opens} (hU : IsAffineOpen U) (f : Γ(X, U))
    [Module Γ(X, U) Γ(M, X.basicOpen f)]
    [IsScalarTower Γ(X, U) Γ(X, X.basicOpen f) Γ(M, X.basicOpen f)] :
    IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) (restrictBasicOpenₗ M f) :=
  haveI := isQuasicoherent_pullback_fromSpec M hU
  isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_of_hP1 M hU
    (isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_isQuasicoherent ((Scheme.Modules.pullback hU.fromSpec).obj M)) f

/-- Restriction maps of the underlying abelian presheaf of a sheaf of modules compose:
restricting `A → B → C` equals the direct restriction `A → C`. -/
private lemma res_res (M : X.Modules) {A B C : X.Opens} (hBA : B ≤ A) (hCB : C ≤ B)
    (hCA : C ≤ A) (x : Γ(M, A)) :
    M.presheaf.map (homOfLE hCB).op (M.presheaf.map (homOfLE hBA).op x)
      = M.presheaf.map (homOfLE hCA).op x := by
  rw [← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, ← Functor.map_comp, ← op_comp]
  rfl

/-- Structure-sheaf version of `res_res`. -/
private lemma resRing_res {A B C : X.Opens} (hBA : B ≤ A) (hCB : C ≤ B)
    (hCA : C ≤ A) (g : Γ(X, A)) :
    X.presheaf.map (homOfLE hCB).op (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE hBA).op g)
      = X.presheaf.map (homOfLE hCA).op g := by
  rw [← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, ← Functor.map_comp, ← op_comp]
  rfl

/-- A section of a sheaf of modules over an open contained in `⊥` vanishes (separation over
the empty cover). -/
private lemma section_eq_zero_of_le_bot (M : X.Modules) {V : X.Opens} (hV : V ≤ ⊥)
    (x : Γ(M, V)) : x = 0 :=
  TopCat.Sheaf.eq_of_locally_eq' (⟨M.presheaf, M.isSheaf⟩ : TopCat.Sheaf Ab X)
    (fun i : PEmpty.{u + 1} => i.elim) V (fun i => i.elim) (hV.trans bot_le) x 0
    (fun i => i.elim)

/-- **Torsion half of qcqs section localization** (Stacks 01P0-style, `exists_of_eq` engine).
For a quasi-coherent sheaf of modules `M` on a scheme `X`, a section `g` of the structure sheaf
over an open `W`, and a *quasi-compact* open `U ≤ W`, any section `x ∈ Γ(M, U)` that restricts
to zero on `U ⊓ D(g)` is killed by a power of `g|_U`. Induction on the compact open `U`
(`compact_open_induction_on`): the affine case is the gap2 keystone
`isLocalizedModule_basicOpen`, and the step glues by sheaf separation over the two-element
cover `{S, V}`. Project-local (Mathlib has no qcqs section-localization). -/
theorem exists_pow_smul_res_eq_zero_of_isCompact
    (M : X.Modules) [M.IsQuasicoherent] {W : X.Opens} (g : Γ(X, W))
    (U : X.Opens) (hU : IsCompact (U : Set X)) :
    ∀ (hUW : U ≤ W) (x : Γ(M, U)),
      M.presheaf.map (homOfLE (inf_le_left : U ⊓ X.basicOpen g ≤ U)).op x = 0 →
      ∃ n : ℕ, X.presheaf.map (homOfLE hUW).op g ^ n • x = 0 := by
  refine compact_open_induction_on (P := fun U => ∀ (hUW : U ≤ W) (x : Γ(M, U)),
      M.presheaf.map (homOfLE (inf_le_left : U ⊓ X.basicOpen g ≤ U)).op x = 0 →
      ∃ n : ℕ, X.presheaf.map (homOfLE hUW).op g ^ n • x = 0) U hU ?_ ?_
  · intro _ x _
    exact ⟨0, by rw [pow_zero, one_smul]; exact section_eq_zero_of_le_bot M le_rfl x⟩
  · intro S hS V IH hUW x hx
    have hSW : S ≤ W := le_sup_left.trans hUW
    have hVW : V.1 ≤ W := le_sup_right.trans hUW
    set gV : Γ(X, V.1) := X.presheaf.map (homOfLE hVW).op g with hgV
    have hB1eq : X.basicOpen gV = V.1 ⊓ X.basicOpen g := X.basicOpen_res g (homOfLE hVW).op
    set xS : Γ(M, S) := M.presheaf.map (homOfLE (le_sup_left : S ≤ S ⊔ V.1)).op x with hxS
    set xV : Γ(M, V.1) := M.presheaf.map (homOfLE (le_sup_right : V.1 ≤ S ⊔ V.1)).op x
      with hxV
    -- the restriction of `xS` to `S ⊓ D(g)` vanishes
    have hxS0 : M.presheaf.map (homOfLE (inf_le_left : S ⊓ X.basicOpen g ≤ S)).op xS = 0 := by
      have h1 := res_res M (le_sup_left : S ≤ S ⊔ V.1)
        (inf_le_left : S ⊓ X.basicOpen g ≤ S) (inf_le_left.trans le_sup_left) x
      have h2 := res_res M (inf_le_left : (S ⊔ V.1) ⊓ X.basicOpen g ≤ S ⊔ V.1)
        (inf_le_inf le_sup_left le_rfl : S ⊓ X.basicOpen g ≤ (S ⊔ V.1) ⊓ X.basicOpen g)
        (inf_le_left.trans le_sup_left) x
      rw [hxS, h1, ← h2, hx, map_zero]
    obtain ⟨n₁, hn₁⟩ := IH hSW xS hxS0
    -- affine keystone on `V`
    letI : Module Γ(X, V.1) Γ(M, X.basicOpen gV) :=
      Module.compHom _ (algebraMap Γ(X, V.1) Γ(X, X.basicOpen gV))
    haveI : IsScalarTower Γ(X, V.1) Γ(X, X.basicOpen gV) Γ(M, X.basicOpen gV) :=

data.content:
The second equality is the universal property of $\widetilde{M}$, see
Lemma \ref{lemma-compare-constructions}. The third equality holds by
definition of $\SheafHom$. The fourth equality is the universal property
of $\widetilde{N}$. Then fifth equality is
Algebra, Lemma \ref{algebra-lemma-hom-from-tensor-product}.
The final equality is the universal property of
$\widetilde{M \otimes_R N}$.
By the Yoneda lemma (Categories, Lemma \ref{categories-lemma-yoneda})
we obtain (1).
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}
\label{lemma-widetilde-pullback}
Let
$(X, \mathcal{O}_X) = (\Spec(S), \mathcal{O}_{\Spec(S)})$,
$(Y, \mathcal{O}_Y) = (\Spec(R), \mathcal{O}_{\Spec(R)})$
be affine schemes.
Let $\psi : (X, \mathcal{O}_X) \to (Y, \mathcal{O}_Y)$ be a
morphism of affine schemes, corresponding to the ring map
$\psi^\sharp : R \to S$ (see Lemma \ref{lemma-category-affine-schemes}).
\begin{enumerate}
\item We have $\psi^* \widetilde M = \widetilde{S \otimes_R M}$
functorially in the $R$-module $M$.
\item We have $\psi_* \widetilde N = \widetilde{N_R}$ functorially
in the $S$-module $N$.
\end{enumerate}
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}
The first assertion follows from the identification in
Lemma \ref{lemma-compare-constructions}
and the result of Modules, Lemma \ref{modules-lemma-restrict-quasi-coherent}.
The second assertion follows from the fact
that $\psi^{-1}(D(f)) = D(\psi^\sharp(f))$ and hence
$$
\psi_* \widetilde N(D(f)) = \widetilde N(D(\psi^\sharp(f))) =
N_{\psi^\sharp(f)} = (N_R)_f = \widetilde{N_R}(D(f))
$$
as desired.
\end{proof}

\noindent
Lemma \ref{lemma-widetilde-pullback} above says in particular
that if you restrict
the sheaf $\widetilde M$ to a standard affine open subspace
$D(f)$, then you get $\widetilde{M_f}$. We will use this from
now on without further mention.

\begin{lemma}
\label{lemma-quasi-coherent-affine}
Let $(X, \mathcal{O}_X) = (\Spec(R), \mathcal{O}_{\Spec(R)})$
be an affine scheme. Let $\mathcal{F}$ be a
quasi-coherent $\mathcal{O}_X$-module. Then
$\mathcal{F}$ is isomorphic to the sheaf associated to
the $R$-module $\Gamma(X, \mathcal{F})$.
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}
Let $\mathcal{F}$ be a quasi-coherent $\mathcal{O}_X$-module.
Since every standard open $D(f)$ is quasi-compact we see that
$X$ is locally quasi-compact, i.e., every point has a fundamental
system of quasi-compact neighbourhoods, see Topology,
Definition \ref{topology-definition-locally-quasi-compact}.
Hence by Modules, Lemma \ref{modules-lemma-quasi-coherent-module}
for every prime $\mathfrak p \subset R$ corresponding to $x \in X$
there exists an open neighbourhood $x \in U \subset X$ such that
$\mathcal{F}|_U$ is isomorphic to the quasi-coherent
sheaf associated to some $\mathcal{O}_X(U)$-module $M$.
In other words, we get an open covering by $U$'s with this property.
By Lemma \ref{lemma-standard-open} for example we can refine this
covering to  a standard open covering.
Thus we get a covering $\Spec(R) = \bigcup D(f_i)$
and $R_{f_i}$-modules $M_i$ and isomorphisms
$\varphi_i : \mathcal{F}|_{D(f_i)} \to \mathcal{F}_{M_i}$
for some $R_{f_i}$-module $M_i$. On the overlaps
we get isomorphisms
$$
\xymatrix{
\mathcal{F}_{M_i}|_{D(f_if_j)}
\ar[rr]^{\varphi_i^{-1}|_{D(f_if_j)}}
& &
\mathcal{F}|_{D(f_if_j)}
\ar[rr]^{\varphi_j|_{D(f_if_j)}}
& &
\mathcal{F}_{M_j}|_{D(f_if_j)}.
}
$$
Let us denote these $\psi_{ij}$. It is clear that
we have the cocycle condition
$$
\psi_{jk}|_{D(f_if_jf_k)}
\circ
\psi_{ij}|_{D(f_if_jf_k)}
=
\psi_{ik}|_{D(f_if_jf_k)}
$$
on triple overlaps.

\medskip\noindent

$$
\item The {\it annihilator of $M$}
is the ideal
$$
\text{Ann}_R(M) = \text{Ann}(M) = \{f \in R \mid fm = 0\ \forall m \in M\}.
$$
\end{enumerate}
\end{definition}

\begin{lemma}
\label{lemma-annihilator-flat-base-change}
Let $R \to S$ be a flat ring map. Let $M$ be an $R$-module and
$m \in M$. Then $\text{Ann}_R(m) S = \text{Ann}_S(m \otimes 1)$.
If $M$ is a finite $R$-module, then
$\text{Ann}_R(M) S = \text{Ann}_S(M \otimes_R S)$.
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}
Set $I = \text{Ann}_R(m)$. By definition there is an exact sequence
$0 \to I \to R \to M$ where the map $R \to M$ sends $f$ to $fm$. Using
flatness we obtain an exact sequence
$0 \to I \otimes_R S \to S \to M \otimes_R S$ which proves the first
assertion. If $m_1, \ldots, m_n$ is a set of generators of $M$
then $\text{Ann}_R(M) = \bigcap \text{Ann}_R(m_i)$. Similarly
$\text{Ann}_S(M \otimes_R S) = \bigcap \text{Ann}_S(m_i \otimes 1)$.
Set $I_i = \text{Ann}_R(m_i)$. Then it suffices to show that
$\bigcap_{i = 1, \ldots, n} (I_i S) = (\bigcap_{i = 1, \ldots, n} I_i)S$.
This is Lemma \ref{lemma-flat-intersect-ideals}.
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}
\label{lemma-support-closed}
Let $R$ be a ring and let $M$ be an $R$-module.
If $M$ is finite, then $\text{Supp}(M)$ is closed.
More precisely, if $I = \text{Ann}(M)$ is the annihilator of $M$, then
$V(I) = \text{Supp}(M)$.
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}
We will show that $V(I) = \text{Supp}(M)$.

\medskip\noindent
Suppose $\mathfrak p \in \text{Supp}(M)$. Then $M_{\mathfrak p} \not = 0$.
Choose an element $m \in M$ whose image in $M_\mathfrak p$ is nonzero.
Then the annihilator of $m$ is contained in $\mathfrak p$ by construction
of the localization $M_\mathfrak p$. Hence
a fortiori $I = \text{Ann}(M)$ must be contained in $\mathfrak p$.

\medskip\noindent
Conversely, suppose that $\mathfrak p \not \in \text{Supp}(M)$.
Then $M_{\mathfrak p} = 0$.
Let $x_1, \ldots, x_r \in M$ be generators.
By Lemma \ref{lemma-localization-colimit} there exists
an $f \in R$, $f\not\in \mathfrak p$ such that
$x_i/1 = 0$ in $M_f$. Hence $f^{n_i} x_i = 0$ for some $n_i \geq 1$.
Hence $f^nM = 0$ for $n = \max\{n_i\}$ as desired.
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}[Annihilator of a finitely generated module commutes with localization]
  \label{lem:annihilator_localization_eq_map}
  \lean{Module.annihilator_isLocalizedModule_eq_map}
  \leanok
  % SOURCE: Stacks, Algebra, section "Supports and annihilators",
  % \texttt{lemma-annihilator-flat-base-change} (read from
  % references/stacks-algebra.tex, L9349-L9368): for a flat ring map R -> S and a
  % finite R-module M, Ann_R(M)S = Ann_S(M ⊗_R S). Localization at a submonoid is
  % the special case S = R_S, M ⊗_R R_S = M_S.
  \textit{Source: Stacks, Algebra, Lemma \texttt{annihilator-flat-base-change}
  (localization instance for finite modules).}
  Let \(R\) be a commutative ring, \(S \subseteq R\) a multiplicative submonoid, and
  \(R_S\) the localization of \(R\) at \(S\). Let \(M\) be a \emph{finitely
  generated} \(R\)-module and let \(g : M \to M_S\) exhibit \(M_S\) as the
  localization of \(M\) at \(S\) (an \(R_S\)-module whose \(R\)-action is the
  restriction of the \(R_S\)-action along \(R \to R_S\)). Then
  \[
    \mathrm{Ann}_{R_S}(M_S) \;=\; \mathrm{Ann}_R(M) \cdot R_S,
  \]
  the extension of the annihilator ideal along the canonical map
  \(R \to R_S\). Finite generation is essential: for a general module only the
  inclusion \(\supseteq\) holds.
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \leanok
  Fix a finite generating set \(m_1, \dots, m_n\) of \(M\). An element of \(R\)
  annihilating every \(m_i\) annihilates all of \(M\): the elements it kills form
  a submodule (they are closed under sums and scalar multiples), and that
  submodule contains the generators.

  (\(\supseteq\)) It suffices that the image of \(\mathrm{Ann}_R(M)\)
  annihilates \(M_S\). Every element of \(M_S\) has the form \(g(m)/s\) with
  \(m \in M\), \(s \in S\); for \(a \in \mathrm{Ann}_R(M)\),
  \((a/1)\cdot(g(m)/s) = g(a \cdot m)/s = 0\).

  (\(\subseteq\)) Let \(y \in \mathrm{Ann}_{R_S}(M_S)\) and write \(y = a/s\)
  with \(a \in R\), \(s \in S\). For each \(i\) the hypothesis gives
  \(y \cdot (g(m_i)/1) = g(a \cdot m_i)/s = 0\) in \(M_S\), so there is
  \(u_i \in S\) with \(u_i \cdot a \cdot m_i = 0\) in \(M\). Put
  \(u := u_1 \cdots u_n \in S\); since each \(u_i\) divides \(u\) in \(S\),
  \((u a) \cdot m_i = 0\) for every \(i\), hence
  \(u a \in \mathrm{Ann}_R(M)\) by the generating-set criterion. Finally
  \[
    \frac{a}{s} \;=\; \frac{ua}{1} \cdot \frac{1}{us}
  \]
  exhibits \(y\) as an \(R_S\)-multiple of the image of
  \(ua \in \mathrm{Ann}_R(M)\), so \(y \in \mathrm{Ann}_R(M) \cdot R_S\).
\end{proof}

% The block below is the module-category layer assembled on top of the topological
% sheaf-congruence def:overEquivalence_sheafCongr: the step-3 equivalence of categories of sheaves
% of modules (def:over_restrict_equiv). Its step-4 functor identification is realized object-wise
% by the slice-touching bridge lem:over_restrict_iso below. Project-bespoke infrastructure (no
% external source).

\begin{definition}[The slice-to-geometric equivalence of module categories (gap1, C, step 3)]
  \label{def:over_restrict_equiv}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.overRestrictEquiv}
  \leanok
  \uses{lem:opens_overEquivalence_mathlib, lem:pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence_mathlib,
    lem:overEquivalence_functor_isContinuous, lem:overEquivalence_inverse_isContinuous}
  Let \(X\) be a scheme and \(U \subseteq X\) an open. The category of sheaves of modules
  over the sliced structure sheaf \(\mathcal{O}_X.\mathrm{over}\,U\) on the slice site
  \(J_X|_U\) is equivalent to the category of sheaves of modules on the open subscheme
  \(U.\mathrm{toScheme}\):
  \[
    \mathrm{SheafOfModules}\bigl(\mathcal{O}_X.\mathrm{over}\,U\bigr)
    \;\simeq\; U.\mathrm{toScheme}.\mathrm{Modules}.
  \]
  The equivalence is obtained by lifting the site equivalence
  \(\mathrm{Opens.overEquivalence}\,U\) (\cref{lem:opens_overEquivalence_mathlib}) — both of
  whose legs are continuous (\cref{lem:overEquivalence_functor_isContinuous},
  \cref{lem:overEquivalence_inverse_isContinuous}) — to module categories through the
  ringed-site transport of \cref{lem:pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence_mathlib}. The two
  ring-sheaf comparison morphisms that transport consumes are the unit of the site
  equivalence whiskered into the sliced structure presheaf, and the identity: the structure
  sheaf of the open subscheme is precisely the transport of the sliced structure sheaf
  across the site equivalence.
\end{definition}

\begin{lemma}[Quasi-coherent modules restrict to a tilde on basic opens of the cover
(gap1, P1)]
  \label{lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_of_quasicoherent}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.isIso_fromTildeΓ_restrict_basicOpen}
  \leanok
  \uses{def:over_restrict_equiv, lem:over_restrict_pullback_iso,
    lem:modules_pullback_mathlib, lem:tilde_gamma_adjunction_mathlib,
    lem:isLocalization_basicOpen_mathlib}
  Let \(M\) be a sheaf of modules on \(\Spec R\) equipped with quasi-coherence data: an
  open cover \(\{X_i\}\) of \(\Spec R\) together with, for each \(i\), a presentation of
  the slice restriction \(M.\mathrm{over}\,X_i\) — an exhibition of it as the cokernel of
  a map between coproducts of copies of the unit module. Let \(r \in R\) with
  \(D(r) \subseteq X_i\) for some \(i\), and let \(W := \iota_{X_i}^{-1}(D(r))\) be the
  corresponding affine open of the subscheme \(Z := X_i\). Then the restriction of \(M\)
  to \(D(r)\) — the pullback of \(M\) to \(Z\), then to \(W\), transported across the
  affine identification \(W \cong \Spec\Gamma(Z, W)\) — has invertible
  tilde--\(\Gamma\) counit (\cref{lem:tilde_gamma_adjunction_mathlib}); that is,
  \(M|_{D(r)}\) is the tilde of its section module over
  \(\Gamma(Z, W) \cong R_r\) (\cref{lem:isLocalization_basicOpen_mathlib}).
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \leanok
  \uses{def:over_restrict_equiv, lem:over_restrict_pullback_iso,
    lem:modules_pullback_mathlib, lem:tilde_gamma_adjunction_mathlib}
  \emph{Step 1 (geometrize the datum).} The quasi-coherence datum supplies a presentation
  of the slice restriction \(M.\mathrm{over}\,X_i\). The slice-to-geometric equivalence
  (\cref{def:over_restrict_equiv}) preserves colimits and carries the unit module to the
  unit module, so the image of this presentation, transported along the isomorphism of
  \cref{lem:over_restrict_pullback_iso}, is a presentation of the geometric pullback
  \(N := \iota_{X_i}^{*} M\) on the scheme \(Z\).

  \emph{Step 2 (restrict to \(W\)).} Restriction to the slice site over \(W\) again
  preserves colimits and the unit, so the global presentation of \(N\) slices to a
  presentation of \(N.\mathrm{over}\,W\); geometrizing across the slice-to-geometric
  equivalence at \(W\) (\cref{def:over_restrict_equiv},
  \cref{lem:over_restrict_pullback_iso}) yields a presentation of the geometric
  restriction \(\iota_W^{*} N\) on the open subscheme \(W\).

  \emph{Step 3 (affine identification).} \(W\) is affine: \(D(r)\) is a basic open of the
  affine scheme \(\Spec R\), and its preimage under the open immersion \(\iota_{X_i}\) is
  again affine because \(D(r)\) is contained in the immersion's range. Pull the
  presentation back across the isomorphism \(W \cong \Spec\Gamma(Z, W)\): the pullback of
  sheaves of modules along an isomorphism of schemes
  (\cref{lem:modules_pullback_mathlib}) preserves colimits, being a left adjoint, and
  carries the unit module to the unit module, since the induced functor on opens sites is
  an equivalence. This lands a global presentation of the transported module on the
  affine scheme \(\Spec\Gamma(Z, W)\).

  \emph{Step 4 (a presented module on \(\Spec S\) is a tilde).} Let \(P\) be a sheaf of
  modules on \(\Spec S\) with a presentation
  \(\mathcal{O}^{\oplus J} \to \mathcal{O}^{\oplus K} \to P \to 0\). The map of coproducts
  of units corresponds under the tilde--\(\Gamma\) adjunction
  (\cref{lem:tilde_gamma_adjunction_mathlib}) to a map of free modules
  \(S^{\oplus J} \to S^{\oplus K}\), because \(\mathcal{O}^{\oplus K}\) is the tilde of
  \(S^{\oplus K}\) and the unit of the adjunction is an isomorphism. Since the tilde
  functor preserves cokernels (it is a left adjoint), \(P\) is isomorphic to the tilde of
  the cokernel of that module map. Finally, a module isomorphic to a tilde has invertible
  counit: on a tilde \(\widetilde{N}\) the triangle identity of the adjunction shows the
  counit is inverse to the tilde of the unit isomorphism \(N \cong
  \Gamma(\widetilde{N}, \top)\), and invertibility of the counit transports across any
  isomorphism of modules by naturality. Applying this to the transported module of
  Step~3 concludes.
\end{proof}

% --- (D) The section-localization descent: the gap1 keystone ---

% NOTE: the lemma STATEMENT is cited to Stacks lemma-invert-f-sections / Hartshorne II.5.3; the proof
% below is the project-internal finite-cover descent (gluing + separatedness + flatness of
% localization) that deliberately does NOT route through the global affine equivalence (which is gap1
% itself), so there is no transcribed `% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF`.

% --- (D, engines) The private sheaf-gluing sub-steps of the cover-form descent (coverage) ---


% --- (D, basic-open cover form) The form gap1 actually consumes ---
input.new_string:
% --- (D) The section-localization descent: the gap1 keystone ---

% NOTE: the descent is stated for an ARBITRARY sheaf of modules under an explicit
% per-cover-element localization hypothesis; it deliberately does NOT route through the global
% affine equivalence (which is gap1 itself). The sheaf-gluing engines (separatedness, unique
% gluing over the finite cover) are steps inside the proofs below.

\begin{lemma}[Section-localization descent from per-cover localization data (gap1, D,
cover form)]
  \label{lem:section_localization_descent_of_cover}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_descent_of_cover}
  \leanok
  \uses{lem:eq_of_locally_eq_mathlib}
  Let \(M\) be a sheaf of modules on \(\Spec R\), \(f \in R\), and
  \(t \subseteq R\) a finite subset generating the unit ideal, so that the basic opens
  \(\{D(r)\}_{r \in t}\) cover \(\Spec R\). Assume the localization datum: for every open
  \(U\) contained in some \(D(r)\) with \(r \in t\), the section restriction
  \[
    \Gamma(M, U) \longrightarrow \Gamma\bigl(M, D(f) \cap U\bigr)
  \]
  exhibits the target as the localization of \(\Gamma(M, U)\) at the powers of \(f\),
  over \(R\). Then the global section restriction
  \(\Gamma(M, \top) \to \Gamma(M, D(f))\) exhibits \(\Gamma(M, D(f))\) as the
  localization \(\Gamma(M, \top)[1/f]\) over \(R\).
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \leanok
  \uses{lem:eq_of_locally_eq_mathlib}
  We verify the three conditions characterizing the localization map.

  \emph{Units.} Every power of \(f\) acts invertibly on \(\Gamma(M, D(f))\): the action
  of \(f\) on sections over \(D(f)\) is through the restriction of \(f\) to
  \(\Gamma(\Spec R, D(f))\), which is a unit there (\(f\) is invertible on its own basic
  open), and multiplication by a unit of the acting ring is bijective. This holds for an
  arbitrary \(M\), without the covering hypothesis.

  \emph{Torsion.} Let \(x \in \Gamma(M, \top)\) restrict to \(0\) on \(D(f)\); we find
  \(n\) with \(f^n \cdot x = 0\) (applied to a difference \(x_1 - x_2\), this is the
  uniqueness condition). For each \(r \in t\), the restriction \(x|_{D(r)}\) restricts to
  \(0\) on \(D(f) \cap D(r)\) (functoriality of restriction through \(D(f)\)); the
  localization datum at \(U = D(r)\) then provides \(k_r\) with
  \(f^{k_r} \cdot x|_{D(r)} = 0\). Take \(n := \max_{r \in t} k_r\); then
  \((f^n \cdot x)|_{D(r)} = f^{\,n-k_r} \cdot \bigl(f^{k_r} \cdot x|_{D(r)}\bigr) = 0\)
  for every \(r\), and since the \(D(r)\) cover \(\Spec R\), sheaf separatedness
  (\cref{lem:eq_of_locally_eq_mathlib}) gives \(f^n \cdot x = 0\).

  \emph{Surjectivity.} Let \(y \in \Gamma(M, D(f))\); we find a global \(x\) and \(n\)
  with \(f^n \cdot y = x|_{D(f)}\). For each \(r \in t\), the localization datum at
  \(U = D(r)\) writes \(f^{m_r} \cdot y|_{D(f) \cap D(r)}\) as the restriction of a
  section \(b_r \in \Gamma(M, D(r))\); normalizing with the common exponent
  \(N := \max_r m_r\) (replace \(b_r\) by \(f^{\,N-m_r} \cdot b_r\)) we may assume
  \(b_r|_{D(f) \cap D(r)} = f^{N} \cdot y|_{D(f) \cap D(r)}\) for every \(r\). On a
  pairwise overlap \(D(r) \cap D(r')\) — an open contained in \(D(r)\) — the difference
  \(b_r - b_{r'}\) restricts to \(0\) on \(D(f) \cap D(r) \cap D(r')\), since both terms
  restrict to \(f^N \cdot y\) there; the localization datum at
  \(U = D(r) \cap D(r')\) provides a power \(f^{P_{r,r'}}\) killing the difference, and
  taking \(P := \max P_{r,r'}\) over the finitely many pairs, the family
  \(\{f^P \cdot b_r\}_{r \in t}\) agrees on all overlaps. By the sheaf axiom (unique
  gluing over the finite cover \(\{D(r)\}_{r \in t}\) of \(\Spec R\)) the family glues to
  a global section \(x \in \Gamma(M, \top)\). Finally
  \(x|_{D(f)} = f^{N+P} \cdot y\): the two sides agree on each member of the cover
  \(\{D(f) \cap D(r)\}_{r \in t}\) of \(D(f)\), so they are equal by separatedness
  (\cref{lem:eq_of_locally_eq_mathlib}).
\end{proof}

% --- (D, basic-open cover form) The form gap1 actually consumes ---

\begin{lemma}[Section-localization descent, basic-open hypothesis form (gap1, D)]
  \label{lem:section_localization_descent_of_basicOpen_cover}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_descent_of_basicOpen_cover}
  \leanok
  \uses{lem:section_localization_descent_of_cover, lem:eq_of_locally_eq_mathlib}
  Let \(M\), \(f\), and \(t\) be as in \cref{lem:section_localization_descent_of_cover},
  but assume the localization datum only for \emph{basic} opens: for every \(s \in R\)
  with \(D(s) \subseteq D(r)\) for some \(r \in t\), the restriction
  \(\Gamma(M, D(s)) \to \Gamma(M, D(f) \cap D(s))\) is the localization of
  \(\Gamma(M, D(s))\) at the powers of \(f\) over \(R\). Then the conclusion of
  \cref{lem:section_localization_descent_of_cover} still holds: the global restriction
  \(\Gamma(M, \top) \to \Gamma(M, D(f))\) is the localization at the powers of \(f\)
  over \(R\).
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \leanok
  \uses{lem:section_localization_descent_of_cover, lem:eq_of_locally_eq_mathlib}
  The proof of \cref{lem:section_localization_descent_of_cover} consults the
  localization datum only at the cover members \(U = D(r)\) (\(r \in t\)) and at their
  pairwise intersections \(U = D(r) \cap D(r') = D(r r')\) — all of them \emph{basic}
  opens contained in a cover member. The datum restricted to basic opens therefore
  suffices, and the same three-part verification applies verbatim. (The restriction of
  the hypothesis matters: the geometric producer below furnishes the localization datum
  only at basic opens, a general open of \(\Spec R\) being neither affine nor
  quasi-compact in general.)
\end{proof}

This subsection builds the sole remaining ingredient of the gap1 keystone descent
\cref{lem:section_localization_descent}: the geometric \emph{producer}
\cref{lem:section_localization_hfr_basicOpen} that manufactures, for a quasi-coherent \(M\) on
\(\Spec R\) and a basic open \(D(s)\) inside a quasi-coherence cover member, the per-cover-element
localization datum \(\mathrm{Hfr}\) the basic-open cover form
\cref{lem:section_localization_descent_of_basicOpen_cover} consumes. The construction transports the
\(R_s\)-level localization living on the affine slice \(D(s) \cong \Spec R_s\) back to an
\(R\)-level statement through the combiner \cref{lem:isLocalizedModule_powers_transport}; the four
sub-lemmas below supply the geometric inputs (an iterated-pullback \(\mathrm{fromTildeΓ}\) iso, the
image/range computations, the \(\top\)-level semilinearity, and the scalar-tower instances).
input.new_string:
This subsection builds the geometric input of the gap1 keystone descent
\cref{lem:section_localization_descent}: the geometric \emph{producer}
\cref{lem:section_localization_hfr_basicOpen} that manufactures, for a quasi-coherent \(M\) on
\(\Spec R\) and a basic open \(D(s)\) inside a quasi-coherence cover member, the per-cover-element
localization datum \(\mathrm{Hfr}\) the basic-open cover form
\cref{lem:section_localization_descent_of_basicOpen_cover} consumes. The construction transports the
\(R_s\)-level localization living on the affine slice \(D(s) \cong \Spec R_s\) back to an
\(R\)-level statement through the combiner \cref{lem:isLocalizedModule_powers_transport}; the
geometric inputs --- the iterated-pullback tilde--\(\Gamma\) isomorphism, the identification of the
immersion's image opens, and the semilinear section comparisons --- enter in the proof of the
producer.

\begin{lemma}[Localization transport across a semilinear section comparison]
  \label{lem:isLocalizedModule_powers_transport}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.isLocalizedModule_powers_transport}
  \leanok
  Let \(R\) be a commutative ring, \(A\) an \(R\)-algebra, \(S\) a commutative ring, and
  \(\sigma : S \xrightarrow{\ \sim\ } A\) a ring isomorphism; let \(f \in R\) and
  \(f' \in S\) with \(\sigma(f')\) equal to the image of \(f\) in \(A\). Let
  \(g : M_1 \to M_2\) be an \(S\)-linear map exhibiting \(M_2\) as the localization of
  \(M_1\) at the powers of \(f'\); let \(N_1, N_2\) be \(A\)-modules, regarded also as
  \(R\)-modules through \(R \to A\); let \(e_1 : M_1 \to N_1\) and \(e_2 : M_2 \to N_2\)
  be additive isomorphisms that are \(\sigma\)-semilinear, i.e.
  \(e_i(a \cdot x) = \sigma(a) \cdot e_i(x)\) for \(a \in S\); and let
  \(h : N_1 \to N_2\) be an \(A\)-linear map intertwining \(g\), i.e.
  \(h \circ e_1 = e_2 \circ g\). Then \(h\), regarded as an \(R\)-linear map, exhibits
  \(N_2\) as the localization of \(N_1\) at the powers of \(f\).
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \leanok
  We check the three localization conditions in two stages.

  \emph{Stage I: crossing \(\sigma\).} The map \(h\) is a localization at the powers of
  \(\sigma(f')\) over \(A\).
  \emph{Units:} for \(k \ge 0\), the semilinearity of \(e_2\) gives that the action of
  \(\sigma(f')^k = \sigma(f'^k)\) on \(N_2\) equals
  \(e_2 \circ (\text{action of } f'^k \text{ on } M_2) \circ e_2^{-1}\), a composite of
  bijections because \(f'^k\) acts invertibly on the localization \(M_2\).
  \emph{Surjectivity:} given \(y \in N_2\), choose \(x \in M_1\) and \(k\) with
  \(f'^k \cdot e_2^{-1}(y) = g(x)\); applying \(e_2\) and semilinearity,
  \(\sigma(f')^k \cdot y = e_2(g(x)) = h(e_1(x))\).
  \emph{Uniqueness:} if \(h(y_1) = h(y_2)\) then, by the intertwining relation,
  \(e_2\bigl(g(e_1^{-1} y_1)\bigr) = e_2\bigl(g(e_1^{-1} y_2)\bigr)\), so
  \(g(e_1^{-1} y_1) = g(e_1^{-1} y_2)\); hence
  \(f'^k \cdot e_1^{-1} y_1 = f'^k \cdot e_1^{-1} y_2\) for some \(k\), and applying
  \(e_1\), \(\sigma(f')^k \cdot y_1 = \sigma(f')^k \cdot y_2\).

  \emph{Stage II: descending to \(R\).} By hypothesis \(\sigma(f')\) is the image of
  \(f\) under \(R \to A\), and by compatibility of the two module structures the action
  of \(f^k\) through \(R\) coincides with the action of the image of \(f^k\) through
  \(A\), on \(N_1\) and on \(N_2\). Each of the three conditions at the powers of \(f\)
  over \(R\) is therefore literally the corresponding Stage-I condition at the powers of
  \(\sigma(f')\) over \(A\): the units condition transfers because the two actions
  coincide; surjectivity reads \(f^k \cdot y = \sigma(f')^k \cdot y = h(x)\); and
  uniqueness likewise.
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}[Section-transport producer: basic-open localization inside a cover member
(gap1, Hfr)]
  \label{lem:section_localization_hfr_basicOpen}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.section_localization_hfr_basicOpen}
  \leanok
  \uses{lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_of_quasicoherent,
    lem:isLocalizedModule_restrict_of_isIso_fromTildeΓ,
    lem:isLocalizedModule_powers_transport,
    lem:modules_restrictFunctorIsoPullback_mathlib, lem:modules_pullback_mathlib}
  Let \(M\) be a sheaf of modules on \(\Spec R\) with quasi-coherence data (an open cover
  \(\{X_i\}\) with per-member presentations, as in
  \cref{lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_of_quasicoherent}), let \(f, s \in R\), and
  suppose \(D(s) \subseteq X_i\) for some \(i\). Then the section restriction
  \[
    \Gamma\bigl(M, D(s)\bigr) \longrightarrow \Gamma\bigl(M, D(f) \cap D(s)\bigr)
  \]
  exhibits the target as the localization of \(\Gamma(M, D(s))\) at the powers of \(f\),
  over \(R\).
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \leanok
  \uses{lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_of_quasicoherent,
    lem:isLocalizedModule_restrict_of_isIso_fromTildeΓ,
    lem:isLocalizedModule_powers_transport,
    lem:modules_restrictFunctorIsoPullback_mathlib, lem:modules_pullback_mathlib}
  Let \(W := \iota_{X_i}^{-1}(D(s))\) be the affine open of the subscheme \(Z := X_i\)
  corresponding to \(D(s)\), put \(S := \Gamma(Z, W)\), and let
  \(j : \Spec S \to \Spec R\) be the composite open immersion
  \(\Spec S \cong W \hookrightarrow Z \hookrightarrow \Spec R\) (the affine
  identification of \(W\) followed by the two canonical immersions). Its image is
  \(D(s)\): the first leg is an isomorphism onto \(W\), and
  \(\iota_{X_i}(W) = X_i \cap D(s) = D(s)\).

  Write \(M' := j^{*} M\) (\cref{lem:modules_pullback_mathlib}). The per-element
  transport \cref{lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_of_quasicoherent} gives invertibility of
  the tilde--\(\Gamma\) counit for the iterated pullback of \(M\) along the three legs of
  \(j\); pullback along a composite agrees with the composite of the pullbacks up to
  canonical natural isomorphism, and invertibility of the counit is invariant under
  isomorphism of modules (the essential image of the tilde functor is closed under
  isomorphism). Hence the counit of \(M'\) is invertible, and by
  \cref{lem:isLocalizedModule_restrict_of_isIso_fromTildeΓ}, for every \(f' \in S\) the
  restriction \(\Gamma(M', \top) \to \Gamma(M', D(f'))\) is the localization at the
  powers of \(f'\) over \(S\).

  Let \(\sigma : S \xrightarrow{\ \sim\ } \Gamma(\Spec R, D(s))\) be the ring comparison
  of the open immersion \(j\) on top sections
  (\(S \cong \Gamma(\Spec S, \top) \cong \Gamma(\Spec R, j(\top))\), and
  \(j(\top) = D(s)\)), and choose \(f' := \sigma^{-1}\bigl(f|_{D(s)}\bigr)\). The image
  \(j\bigl(D(f')\bigr)\) is the locus of \(D(s)\) where \(\sigma(f') = f|_{D(s)}\) is
  invertible, that is, \(j\bigl(D(f')\bigr) = D(f) \cap D(s)\).

  Since \(j\) is an open immersion, applying the section functor to the identification of
  the pullback with the restriction (\cref{lem:modules_restrictFunctorIsoPullback_mathlib})
  gives additive isomorphisms
  \(e_1 : \Gamma(M', \top) \cong \Gamma\bigl(M, D(s)\bigr)\) and
  \(e_2 : \Gamma(M', D(f')) \cong \Gamma\bigl(M, D(f) \cap D(s)\bigr)\) which intertwine
  the two restriction maps (naturality of the identification in the open) and are
  \(\sigma\)-semilinear over the structure-sheaf comparisons: the action on the sections
  of the pullback is through the immersion's section-ring isomorphism.

  Finally apply the combiner \cref{lem:isLocalizedModule_powers_transport} with
  \(A := \Gamma(\Spec R, D(s))\) — an \(R\)-algebra through restriction of global
  sections — the isomorphism \(\sigma\), the elements \(f\) and \(f'\) (so that
  \(\sigma(f')\) is the image of \(f\) in \(A\)), the localization
  \(g := \bigl(\Gamma(M', \top) \to \Gamma(M', D(f'))\bigr)\), the comparisons
  \(e_1, e_2\), and \(h := \bigl(\Gamma(M, D(s)) \to \Gamma(M, D(f) \cap D(s))\bigr)\):
  the conclusion is that \(h\) is the localization at the powers of \(f\) over \(R\).
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}[Section-localization descent for quasi-coherent modules (gap1 keystone)]
  \label{lem:section_localization_descent}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_descent}
  \leanok
  \uses{lem:section_localization_descent_of_basicOpen_cover,
    lem:section_localization_hfr_basicOpen}
  For a quasi-coherent sheaf of modules \(M\) on \(\Spec R\) and \(f \in R\), the section
  restriction \(\Gamma(M, \top) \to \Gamma(M, D(f))\) exhibits the target as the
  localization \(\Gamma(M, \top)[1/f]\) over \(R\). This is the statement of
  \cref{lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen} at the affine open \(\top\) of
  \(\Spec R\), proved here by finite-cover descent --- not through the affine
  equivalence of quasi-coherent modules with modules, which is instead \emph{derived}
  from this lemma in \cref{lem:qcoh_affine_isIso_fromTildeΓ}.
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \leanok
  \uses{lem:section_localization_descent_of_basicOpen_cover,
    lem:section_localization_hfr_basicOpen}
  Quasi-coherence provides a cover \(\{X_i\}\) of \(\Spec R\) with per-member
  presentations. Refine it to a finite basic-open cover: the basic opens form a basis of
  \(\Spec R\), so every point has a basic-open neighbourhood contained in some \(X_i\);
  quasi-compactness of \(\Spec R\) extracts a finite family \(t \subseteq R\) with each
  \(D(r)\), \(r \in t\), contained in some \(X_i\), and covering is equivalent to \(t\)
  generating the unit ideal. For every basic open \(D(u)\) contained in some \(D(r)\)
  with \(r \in t\) — hence contained in a cover member \(X_i\) — the producer
  \cref{lem:section_localization_hfr_basicOpen} supplies the localization datum at
  \(D(u)\). The basic-open cover form
  \cref{lem:section_localization_descent_of_basicOpen_cover} then yields the conclusion.
\end{proof}

input.new_string:
This subsection records the bridge \emph{section localization on every basic open}
\(\Rightarrow\) \emph{invertible tilde--\(\Gamma\) counit}: given that the section
restriction localizes on every basic open, the tilde--\(\Gamma\) counit is an
isomorphism. Together with its converse
\cref{lem:isLocalizedModule_restrict_of_isIso_fromTildeΓ} it assembles the equivalence
\cref{lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_iff_isLocalizedModule_restrict}, which is exactly what makes G1-core
(\cref{lem:qcoh_affine_section_localization}) and gap1
(\cref{lem:qcoh_affine_isIso_fromTildeΓ}) interderivable -- the route by which G1-core is obtained as
a corollary of gap1. The supporting Mathlib facts enter through the tilde--\(\Gamma\)
adjunction anchor (\cref{lem:tilde_gamma_adjunction_mathlib}).

% --- Project-original G1-assemble nodes (Lean realizations) ---

\begin{lemma}[Basic-open section localization for a module with invertible
tilde--\texorpdfstring{\(\Gamma\)}{Gamma} counit]
  \label{lem:isLocalizedModule_restrict_of_isIso_fromTildeΓ}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.isLocalizedModule_restrict_of_isIso_fromTildeΓ}
  \leanok
  \uses{lem:tilde_gamma_adjunction_mathlib}
  % SOURCE: Stacks, Schemes, Lemma \texttt{lemma-widetilde-pullback} and the remark
  % following it (restricting a tilde to a standard open D(f) yields the tilde of the
  % localized module; read from references/stacks-schemes.tex, L1242-L1274).
  \textit{Source: Stacks, Schemes, Lemma \texttt{widetilde-pullback} (sections of a
  tilde over a basic open are the localized module).}
  Let \(M\) be a sheaf of modules on \(\Spec R\) whose tilde--\(\Gamma\) counit
  \(\widetilde{\Gamma(M, \top)} \to M\) (\cref{lem:tilde_gamma_adjunction_mathlib}) is an
  isomorphism. Then for every \(f \in R\) the section restriction
  \(\Gamma(M, \top) \to \Gamma(M, D(f))\) exhibits the target as the localization of
  \(\Gamma(M, \top)\) at the powers of \(f\), over \(R\).
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \leanok
  \uses{lem:tilde_gamma_adjunction_mathlib}
  First let \(M = \widetilde{N}\) be a tilde. The unit
  \(N \to \Gamma(\widetilde{N}, \top)\) of the adjunction
  (\cref{lem:tilde_gamma_adjunction_mathlib}) is an isomorphism, and the canonical map
  \(N \to \Gamma(\widetilde{N}, D(f))\) exhibits the target as the localized module
  \(N_f\) — the sections of a tilde over a basic open are the localization. The
  canonical map factors as the unit followed by the section restriction, so the
  restriction \(\Gamma(\widetilde{N}, \top) \to \Gamma(\widetilde{N}, D(f))\) is the
  composite of the inverse unit isomorphism with a localization map, hence itself a
  localization at the powers of \(f\).

  For general \(M\), put \(N := \Gamma(M, \top)\). The counit isomorphism
  \(\widetilde{N} \cong M\) induces, naturally in the open, \(R\)-linear isomorphisms
  \(\Gamma(\widetilde{N}, \top) \cong \Gamma(M, \top)\) and
  \(\Gamma(\widetilde{N}, D(f)) \cong \Gamma(M, D(f))\) intertwining the two restriction
  maps. Pre- and post-composing a localization map with \(R\)-linear isomorphisms
  compatible in this way preserves the localization property.
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}[Characterization of the invertible
tilde--\texorpdfstring{\(\Gamma\)}{Gamma} counit by section localization]
  \label{lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_iff_isLocalizedModule_restrict}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.isIso_fromTildeΓ_iff_isLocalizedModule_restrict}
  \leanok
  \uses{lem:isLocalizedModule_restrict_of_isIso_fromTildeΓ,
    lem:tilde_gamma_adjunction_mathlib}
  For a sheaf of modules \(M\) on \(\Spec R\), the tilde--\(\Gamma\) counit
  \(\widetilde{\Gamma(M, \top)} \to M\) is an isomorphism if and only if for every
  \(f \in R\) the section restriction \(\Gamma(M, \top) \to \Gamma(M, D(f))\) exhibits
  the target as the localization of \(\Gamma(M, \top)\) at the powers of \(f\) over
  \(R\).
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \leanok
  \uses{lem:isLocalizedModule_restrict_of_isIso_fromTildeΓ,
    lem:tilde_gamma_adjunction_mathlib}
  The forward direction is \cref{lem:isLocalizedModule_restrict_of_isIso_fromTildeΓ}.

  For the converse, write \(N := \Gamma(M, \top)\) and let
  \(\varepsilon : \widetilde{N} \to M\) be the counit. Fix \(f \in R\). The
  \(D(f)\)-component of \(\varepsilon\) is a map between two localizations of \(N\) at
  the powers of \(f\): the source \(\Gamma(\widetilde{N}, D(f))\) receives the canonical
  map from \(N\) (sections of a tilde over a basic open, as in the forward direction),
  and the target \(\Gamma(M, D(f))\) receives the restriction
  \(N = \Gamma(M, \top) \to \Gamma(M, D(f))\), a localization by hypothesis. The
  component intertwines these two maps out of \(N\): the counit composed with the
  canonical section map is the section restriction, by the compatibility of the counit
  with the adjunction unit (\cref{lem:tilde_gamma_adjunction_mathlib}) and naturality.
  A map between two localizations of the same module at the same multiplicative set
  commuting with the two localization maps is the canonical isomorphism between them,
  hence bijective. Thus \(\varepsilon\) is an isomorphism on every basic open.

  Since the basic opens form a basis of \(\Spec R\), \(\varepsilon\) is an isomorphism
  on every stalk: injective, because a germ in the kernel is represented by a section
  over some basic neighbourhood killed by \(\varepsilon\) there, hence zero; surjective,
  because every germ of \(M\) is the germ of a section over some basic open, which is
  hit by the corresponding section of \(\widetilde{N}\). A morphism of sheaves that is
  an isomorphism on all stalks is an isomorphism, and an isomorphism of the underlying
  sheaves underlying a morphism of sheaves of modules is an isomorphism of sheaves of
  modules.
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}[A quasi-coherent module on an affine scheme is the tilde of its sections
(gap1, Stacks 01I8)]
  \label{lem:qcoh_affine_isIso_fromTildeΓ}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_isQuasicoherent}
  \leanok
  \uses{lem:section_localization_descent,
    lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_iff_isLocalizedModule_restrict}
  % SOURCE: Stacks, Schemes, Lemma \texttt{lemma-quasi-coherent-affine} (read from
  % references/stacks-schemes.tex, L1278-L1340): a quasi-coherent module on Spec(R) is
  % isomorphic to the sheaf associated to the R-module of its global sections.
  \textit{Source: Stacks~\href{https://stacks.math.columbia.edu/tag/01I8}{01I8}
  (\texttt{lemma-quasi-coherent-affine}).}
  For a quasi-coherent sheaf of modules \(M\) on \(\Spec R\), the tilde--\(\Gamma\)
  counit \(\widetilde{\Gamma(M, \top)} \to M\) is an isomorphism; equivalently, \(M\)
  lies in the essential image of the tilde functor, i.e. \(M\) is isomorphic to the
  tilde of an \(R\)-module.
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \leanok
  \uses{lem:section_localization_descent,
    lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_iff_isLocalizedModule_restrict}
  By the keystone descent \cref{lem:section_localization_descent}, every basic-open
  section restriction \(\Gamma(M, \top) \to \Gamma(M, D(f))\) is the localization at the
  powers of \(f\). The reverse implication of
  \cref{lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_iff_isLocalizedModule_restrict} concludes.
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}[Affine section localization for quasi-coherent modules (G1-core)]
  \label{lem:qcoh_affine_section_localization}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_of_isQuasicoherent}
  \leanok
  \uses{lem:qcoh_affine_isIso_fromTildeΓ,
    lem:isLocalizedModule_restrict_of_isIso_fromTildeΓ}
  For a quasi-coherent sheaf of modules \(M\) on \(\Spec R\) and \(f \in R\), the
  section restriction \(\Gamma(M, \top) \to \Gamma(M, D(f))\) exhibits the target as the
  localization \(\Gamma(M, \top)[1/f]\) over \(R\). This is the statement of
  \cref{lem:section_localization_descent} in its clean named form — obtained here as a
  corollary of gap1 rather than by re-running the descent — and the affine instance
  (\(X = \Spec R\), \(U = \top\)) of the general-scheme keystone
  \cref{lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen}.
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \leanok
  \uses{lem:qcoh_affine_isIso_fromTildeΓ,
    lem:isLocalizedModule_restrict_of_isIso_fromTildeΓ}
  Gap1 (\cref{lem:qcoh_affine_isIso_fromTildeΓ}) makes the tilde--\(\Gamma\) counit of
  \(M\) an isomorphism, and
  \cref{lem:isLocalizedModule_restrict_of_isIso_fromTildeΓ} applies.
\end{proof}

\textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 5, Theorem (Grothendieck), Theorem
  (Altman--Kleiman) (FGA Explained Ch.~5); cf.\ Grothendieck, FGA TDTE-IV.}
  Let \(S\) be a noetherian scheme, \(\pi : X \to S\) a projective morphism,
  \(L\) a relatively very ample line bundle on \(X\), \(E\) a coherent
  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module, and \(\Phi \in \mathbb{Q}[\lambda]\). Then the
  functor \(\Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S}\) of \cref{def:quot_functor} is
  representable by a projective \(S\)-scheme \(\Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S}\),
  equipped with a universal quotient
  \[
    q^{\mathrm{univ}} :
    \pi^*_{\Quot} E \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{F}^{\mathrm{univ}}
    \quad \text{on } X \times_S \Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S},
  \]
  flat over \(\Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S}\) with constant Hilbert polynomial
  \(\Phi\) on every fiber. The Hilbert scheme is the special case
  \(E = \mathcal{O}_X\): \(\Hilb^{\Phi,L}_{X/S} = \Quot^{\Phi,L}_{\mathcal{O}_X/X/S}\)
  represents the functor of \(T\)-flat closed subschemes
  \(Y \subset X_T\) with fiberwise Hilbert polynomial \(\Phi\).
\end{theorem}

2857	  Let \(M\) be a sheaf of modules on \(\Spec R\) equipped with quasi-coherence data: an
2858	  open cover \(\{X_i\}\) of \(\Spec R\) together with, for each \(i\), a presentation of
2859	  the slice restriction \(M.\mathrm{over}\,X_i\) — an exhibition of it as the cokernel of
2860	  a map between coproducts of copies of the unit module. Let \(r \in R\) with
2861	  \(D(r) \subseteq X_i\) for some \(i\), and let \(W := \iota_{X_i}^{-1}(D(r))\) be the
2862	  corresponding affine open of the subscheme \(Z := X_i\). Then the restriction of \(M\)
2863	  to \(D(r)\) — the pullback of \(M\) to \(Z\), then to \(W\), transported across the
2864	  affine identification \(W \cong \Spec\Gamma(Z, W)\) — has invertible
2865	  tilde--\(\Gamma\) counit (\cref{lem:tilde_gamma_adjunction_mathlib}); that is,
2866	  \(M|_{D(r)}\) is the tilde of its section module over
2867	  \(\Gamma(Z, W) \cong R_r\) (\cref{lem:isLocalization_basicOpen_mathlib}).

2874	  of the slice restriction \(M.\mathrm{over}\,X_i\). The slice-to-geometric equivalence
2875	  (\cref{def:over_restrict_equiv}) preserves colimits and carries the unit module to the
2876	  unit module, so the image of this presentation, transported along the isomorphism of
2877	  \cref{lem:over_restrict_pullback_iso}, is a presentation of the geometric pullback

2880	  \emph{Step 2 (restrict to \(W\)).} Restriction to the slice site over \(W\) again
2881	  preserves colimits and the unit, so the global presentation of \(N\) slices to a

2888	  affine scheme \(\Spec R\), and its preimage under the open immersion \(\iota_{X_i}\) is
2889	  again affine because \(D(r)\) is contained in the immersion's range. Pull the
2890	  presentation back across the isomorphism \(W \cong \Spec\Gamma(Z, W)\): the pullback of
2891	  sheaves of modules along an isomorphism of schemes
2892	  (\cref{lem:modules_pullback_mathlib}) preserves colimits, being a left adjoint, and
2893	  carries the unit module to the unit module, since the induced functor on opens sites is
2894	  an equivalence. This lands a global presentation of the transported module on the
2895	  affine scheme \(\Spec\Gamma(Z, W)\).
2896	
2897	  \emph{Step 4 (a presented module on \(\Spec S\) is a tilde).} Let \(P\) be a sheaf of
2898	  modules on \(\Spec S\) with a presentation
2899	  \(\mathcal{O}^{\oplus J} \to \mathcal{O}^{\oplus K} \to P \to 0\). The map of coproducts
2900	  of units corresponds under the tilde--\(\Gamma\) adjunction
2901	  (\cref{lem:tilde_gamma_adjunction_mathlib}) to a map of free modules
2902	  \(S^{\oplus J} \to S^{\oplus K}\), because \(\mathcal{O}^{\oplus K}\) is the tilde of
2903	  \(S^{\oplus K}\) and the unit of the adjunction is an isomorphism. Since the tilde
2904	  functor preserves cokernels (it is a left adjoint), \(P\) is isomorphic to the tilde of
2905	  the cokernel of that module map. Finally, a module isomorphic to a tilde has invertible
2906	  counit: on a tilde \(\widetilde{N}\) the triangle identity of the adjunction shows the
2907	  counit is inverse to the tilde of the unit isomorphism \(N \cong
2908	  \Gamma(\widetilde{N}, \top)\), and invertibility of the counit transports across any
2909	  isomorphism of modules by naturality. Applying this to the transported module of
2910	  Step~3 concludes.
2911	\end{proof}
2912	
2913	% --- (D) The section-localization descent: the gap1 keystone ---
2914	
2915	% NOTE: the descent is stated for an ARBITRARY sheaf of modules under an explicit
2916	% per-cover-element localization hypothesis; it deliberately does NOT route through the global
2917	% affine equivalence (which is gap1 itself). The sheaf-gluing engines (separatedness, unique
2918	% gluing over the finite cover) are steps inside the proofs below.
2919	
2920	\begin{lemma}[Section-localization descent from per-cover localization data (gap1, D,
2921	cover form)]
2922	  \label{lem:section_localization_descent_of_cover}

2920	\begin{lemma}[Section-localization descent from per-cover localization data (gap1, D,
2921	cover form)]
2922	  \label{lem:section_localization_descent_of_cover}
2923	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_descent_of_cover}
2924	  \leanok
2925	  \uses{lem:eq_of_locally_eq_mathlib}
2926	  Let \(M\) be a sheaf of modules on \(\Spec R\), \(f \in R\), and
2927	  \(t \subseteq R\) a finite subset generating the unit ideal, so that the basic opens
2928	  \(\{D(r)\}_{r \in t}\) cover \(\Spec R\). Assume the localization datum: for every open
2929	  \(U\) contained in some \(D(r)\) with \(r \in t\), the section restriction
2930	  \[
2931	    \Gamma(M, U) \longrightarrow \Gamma\bigl(M, D(f) \cap U\bigr)
2932	  \]
2933	  exhibits the target as the localization of \(\Gamma(M, U)\) at the powers of \(f\),
2934	  over \(R\). Then the global section restriction
2935	  \(\Gamma(M, \top) \to \Gamma(M, D(f))\) exhibits \(\Gamma(M, D(f))\) as the
2936	  localization \(\Gamma(M, \top)[1/f]\) over \(R\).
2937	\end{lemma}
2938	\begin{proof}
2939	  \leanok
2940	  \uses{lem:eq_of_locally_eq_mathlib}
2941	  We verify the three conditions characterizing the localization map.
2942	
2943	  \emph{Units.} Every power of \(f\) acts invertibly on \(\Gamma(M, D(f))\): the action
2944	  of \(f\) on sections over \(D(f)\) is through the restriction of \(f\) to
2945	  \(\Gamma(\Spec R, D(f))\), which is a unit there (\(f\) is invertible on its own basic
2946	  open), and multiplication by a unit of the acting ring is bijective. This holds for an
2947	  arbitrary \(M\), without the covering hypothesis.
2948	
2949	  \emph{Torsion.} Let \(x \in \Gamma(M, \top)\) restrict to \(0\) on \(D(f)\); we find
2950	  \(n\) with \(f^n \cdot x = 0\) (applied to a difference \(x_1 - x_2\), this is the
2951	  uniqueness condition). For each \(r \in t\), the restriction \(x|_{D(r)}\) restricts to
2952	  \(0\) on \(D(f) \cap D(r)\) (functoriality of restriction through \(D(f)\)); the
2953	  localization datum at \(U = D(r)\) then provides \(k_r\) with
2954	  \(f^{k_r} \cdot x|_{D(r)} = 0\). Take \(n := \max_{r \in t} k_r\); then
2955	  \((f^n \cdot x)|_{D(r)} = f^{\,n-k_r} \cdot \bigl(f^{k_r} \cdot x|_{D(r)}\bigr) = 0\)
2956	  for every \(r\), and since the \(D(r)\) cover \(\Spec R\), sheaf separatedness
2957	  (\cref{lem:eq_of_locally_eq_mathlib}) gives \(f^n \cdot x = 0\).
2958	
2959	  \emph{Surjectivity.} Let \(y \in \Gamma(M, D(f))\); we find a global \(x\) and \(n\)
2960	  with \(f^n \cdot y = x|_{D(f)}\). For each \(r \in t\), the localization datum at
2961	  \(U = D(r)\) writes \(f^{m_r} \cdot y|_{D(f) \cap D(r)}\) as the restriction of a
2962	  section \(b_r \in \Gamma(M, D(r))\); normalizing with the common exponent
2963	  \(N := \max_r m_r\) (replace \(b_r\) by \(f^{\,N-m_r} \cdot b_r\)) we may assume
2964	  \(b_r|_{D(f) \cap D(r)} = f^{N} \cdot y|_{D(f) \cap D(r)}\) for every \(r\). On a
2965	  pairwise overlap \(D(r) \cap D(r')\) — an open contained in \(D(r)\) — the difference
2966	  \(b_r - b_{r'}\) restricts to \(0\) on \(D(f) \cap D(r) \cap D(r')\), since both terms
2967	  restrict to \(f^N \cdot y\) there; the localization datum at
2968	  \(U = D(r) \cap D(r')\) provides a power \(f^{P_{r,r'}}\) killing the difference, and
2969	  taking \(P := \max P_{r,r'}\) over the finitely many pairs, the family
2970	  \(\{f^P \cdot b_r\}_{r \in t}\) agrees on all overlaps. By the sheaf axiom (unique
2971	  gluing over the finite cover \(\{D(r)\}_{r \in t}\) of \(\Spec R\)) the family glues to
2972	  a global section \(x \in \Gamma(M, \top)\). Finally
2973	  \(x|_{D(f)} = f^{N+P} \cdot y\): the two sides agree on each member of the cover
2974	  \(\{D(f) \cap D(r)\}_{r \in t}\) of \(D(f)\), so they are equal by separatedness
2975	  (\cref{lem:eq_of_locally_eq_mathlib}).
2976	\end{proof}
2977	
2978	% --- (D, basic-open cover form) The form gap1 actually consumes ---
2979	
2980	\begin{lemma}[Section-localization descent, basic-open hypothesis form (gap1, D)]
2981	  \label{lem:section_localization_descent_of_basicOpen_cover}
2982	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_descent_of_basicOpen_cover}
2983	  \leanok
2984	  \uses{lem:section_localization_descent_of_cover, lem:eq_of_locally_eq_mathlib}
2985	  Let \(M\), \(f\), and \(t\) be as in \cref{lem:section_localization_descent_of_cover},
2986	  but assume the localization datum only for \emph{basic} opens: for every \(s \in R\)
2987	  with \(D(s) \subseteq D(r)\) for some \(r \in t\), the restriction
2988	  \(\Gamma(M, D(s)) \to \Gamma(M, D(f) \cap D(s))\) is the localization of
2989	  \(\Gamma(M, D(s))\) at the powers of \(f\) over \(R\). Then the conclusion of
2990	  \cref{lem:section_localization_descent_of_cover} still holds: the global restriction
2991	  \(\Gamma(M, \top) \to \Gamma(M, D(f))\) is the localization at the powers of \(f\)
2992	  over \(R\).
2993	\end{lemma}
2994	\begin{proof}
2995	  \leanok
2996	  \uses{lem:section_localization_descent_of_cover, lem:eq_of_locally_eq_mathlib}
2997	  The proof of \cref{lem:section_localization_descent_of_cover} consults the
2998	  localization datum only at the cover members \(U = D(r)\) (\(r \in t\)) and at their
2999	  pairwise intersections \(U = D(r) \cap D(r') = D(r r')\) — all of them \emph{basic}
3000	  opens contained in a cover member. The datum restricted to basic opens therefore
3001	  suffices, and the same three-part verification applies verbatim. (The restriction of
3002	  the hypothesis matters: the geometric producer below furnishes the localization datum
3003	  only at basic opens, a general open of \(\Spec R\) being neither affine nor
3004	  quasi-compact in general.)
3005	\end{proof}
3006	
3007	
3008	\subsection{Section-transport producer for the basic-open Hfr}
3009	
3010	This subsection builds the geometric input of the gap1 keystone descent
3011	\cref{lem:section_localization_descent}: the geometric \emph{producer}
3012	\cref{lem:section_localization_hfr_basicOpen} that manufactures, for a quasi-coherent \(M\) on
3013	\(\Spec R\) and a basic open \(D(s)\) inside a quasi-coherence cover member, the per-cover-element
3014	localization datum \(\mathrm{Hfr}\) the basic-open cover form
3015	\cref{lem:section_localization_descent_of_basicOpen_cover} consumes. The construction transports the
3016	\(R_s\)-level localization living on the affine slice \(D(s) \cong \Spec R_s\) back to an
3017	\(R\)-level statement through the combiner \cref{lem:isLocalizedModule_powers_transport}; the
3018	geometric inputs --- the iterated-pullback tilde--\(\Gamma\) isomorphism, the identification of the
3019	immersion's image opens, and the semilinear section comparisons --- enter in the proof of the
3020	producer.
3021	
3022	\begin{lemma}[Localization transport across a semilinear section comparison]
3023	  \label{lem:isLocalizedModule_powers_transport}
3024	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.isLocalizedModule_powers_transport}
3025	  \leanok
3026	  Let \(R\) be a commutative ring, \(A\) an \(R\)-algebra, \(S\) a commutative ring, and
3027	  \(\sigma : S \xrightarrow{\ \sim\ } A\) a ring isomorphism; let \(f \in R\) and
3028	  \(f' \in S\) with \(\sigma(f')\) equal to the image of \(f\) in \(A\). Let
3029	  \(g : M_1 \to M_2\) be an \(S\)-linear map exhibiting \(M_2\) as the localization of

3029	  \(g : M_1 \to M_2\) be an \(S\)-linear map exhibiting \(M_2\) as the localization of
3030	  \(M_1\) at the powers of \(f'\); let \(N_1, N_2\) be \(A\)-modules, regarded also as
3031	  \(R\)-modules through \(R \to A\); let \(e_1 : M_1 \to N_1\) and \(e_2 : M_2 \to N_2\)
3032	  be additive isomorphisms that are \(\sigma\)-semilinear, i.e.
3033	  \(e_i(a \cdot x) = \sigma(a) \cdot e_i(x)\) for \(a \in S\); and let
3034	  \(h : N_1 \to N_2\) be an \(A\)-linear map intertwining \(g\), i.e.
3035	  \(h \circ e_1 = e_2 \circ g\). Then \(h\), regarded as an \(R\)-linear map, exhibits
3036	  \(N_2\) as the localization of \(N_1\) at the powers of \(f\).
3037	\end{lemma}
3038	\begin{proof}
3039	  \leanok
3040	  We check the three localization conditions in two stages.
3041	
3042	  \emph{Stage I: crossing \(\sigma\).} The map \(h\) is a localization at the powers of
3043	  \(\sigma(f')\) over \(A\).
3044	  \emph{Units:} for \(k \ge 0\), the semilinearity of \(e_2\) gives that the action of
3045	  \(\sigma(f')^k = \sigma(f'^k)\) on \(N_2\) equals
3046	  \(e_2 \circ (\text{action of } f'^k \text{ on } M_2) \circ e_2^{-1}\), a composite of
3047	  bijections because \(f'^k\) acts invertibly on the localization \(M_2\).
3048	  \emph{Surjectivity:} given \(y \in N_2\), choose \(x \in M_1\) and \(k\) with
3049	  \(f'^k \cdot e_2^{-1}(y) = g(x)\); applying \(e_2\) and semilinearity,
3050	  \(\sigma(f')^k \cdot y = e_2(g(x)) = h(e_1(x))\).
3051	  \emph{Uniqueness:} if \(h(y_1) = h(y_2)\) then, by the intertwining relation,
3052	  \(e_2\bigl(g(e_1^{-1} y_1)\bigr) = e_2\bigl(g(e_1^{-1} y_2)\bigr)\), so
3053	  \(g(e_1^{-1} y_1) = g(e_1^{-1} y_2)\); hence
3054	  \(f'^k \cdot e_1^{-1} y_1 = f'^k \cdot e_1^{-1} y_2\) for some \(k\), and applying
3055	  \(e_1\), \(\sigma(f')^k \cdot y_1 = \sigma(f')^k \cdot y_2\).
3056	
3057	  \emph{Stage II: descending to \(R\).} By hypothesis \(\sigma(f')\) is the image of
3058	  \(f\) under \(R \to A\), and by compatibility of the two module structures the action
3059	  of \(f^k\) through \(R\) coincides with the action of the image of \(f^k\) through
3060	  \(A\), on \(N_1\) and on \(N_2\). Each of the three conditions at the powers of \(f\)
3061	  over \(R\) is therefore literally the corresponding Stage-I condition at the powers of
3062	  \(\sigma(f')\) over \(A\): the units condition transfers because the two actions
3063	  coincide; surjectivity reads \(f^k \cdot y = \sigma(f')^k \cdot y = h(x)\); and
3064	  uniqueness likewise.
3065	\end{proof}
3066	
3067	\begin{lemma}[Section-transport producer: basic-open localization inside a cover member
3068	(gap1, Hfr)]
3069	  \label{lem:section_localization_hfr_basicOpen}
3070	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.section_localization_hfr_basicOpen}
3071	  \leanok
3072	  \uses{lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_of_quasicoherent,
3073	    lem:isLocalizedModule_restrict_of_isIso_fromTildeΓ,
3074	    lem:isLocalizedModule_powers_transport,
3075	    lem:modules_restrictFunctorIsoPullback_mathlib, lem:modules_pullback_mathlib}
3076	  Let \(M\) be a sheaf of modules on \(\Spec R\) with quasi-coherence data (an open cover
3077	  \(\{X_i\}\) with per-member presentations, as in
3078	  \cref{lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_of_quasicoherent}), let \(f, s \in R\), and
3079	  suppose \(D(s) \subseteq X_i\) for some \(i\). Then the section restriction
3080	  \[
3081	    \Gamma\bigl(M, D(s)\bigr) \longrightarrow \Gamma\bigl(M, D(f) \cap D(s)\bigr)
3082	  \]
3083	  exhibits the target as the localization of \(\Gamma(M, D(s))\) at the powers of \(f\),
3084	  over \(R\).
3085	\end{lemma}
3086	\begin{proof}
3087	  \leanok
3088	  \uses{lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_of_quasicoherent,
3089	    lem:isLocalizedModule_restrict_of_isIso_fromTildeΓ,
3090	    lem:isLocalizedModule_powers_transport,
3091	    lem:modules_restrictFunctorIsoPullback_mathlib, lem:modules_pullback_mathlib}
3092	  Let \(W := \iota_{X_i}^{-1}(D(s))\) be the affine open of the subscheme \(Z := X_i\)
3093	  corresponding to \(D(s)\), put \(S := \Gamma(Z, W)\), and let
3094	  \(j : \Spec S \to \Spec R\) be the composite open immersion
3095	  \(\Spec S \cong W \hookrightarrow Z \hookrightarrow \Spec R\) (the affine
3096	  identification of \(W\) followed by the two canonical immersions). Its image is
3097	  \(D(s)\): the first leg is an isomorphism onto \(W\), and
3098	  \(\iota_{X_i}(W) = X_i \cap D(s) = D(s)\).
3099	
3100	  Write \(M' := j^{*} M\) (\cref{lem:modules_pullback_mathlib}). The per-element
3101	  transport \cref{lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_of_quasicoherent} gives invertibility of
3102	  the tilde--\(\Gamma\) counit for the iterated pullback of \(M\) along the three legs of
3103	  \(j\); pullback along a composite agrees with the composite of the pullbacks up to
3104	  canonical natural isomorphism, and invertibility of the counit is invariant under
3105	  isomorphism of modules (the essential image of the tilde functor is closed under
3106	  isomorphism). Hence the counit of \(M'\) is invertible, and by
3107	  \cref{lem:isLocalizedModule_restrict_of_isIso_fromTildeΓ}, for every \(f' \in S\) the
3108	  restriction \(\Gamma(M', \top) \to \Gamma(M', D(f'))\) is the localization at the
3109	  powers of \(f'\) over \(S\).
3110	
3111	  Let \(\sigma : S \xrightarrow{\ \sim\ } \Gamma(\Spec R, D(s))\) be the ring comparison
3112	  of the open immersion \(j\) on top sections
3113	  (\(S \cong \Gamma(\Spec S, \top) \cong \Gamma(\Spec R, j(\top))\), and
3114	  \(j(\top) = D(s)\)), and choose \(f' := \sigma^{-1}\bigl(f|_{D(s)}\bigr)\). The image
3115	  \(j\bigl(D(f')\bigr)\) is the locus of \(D(s)\) where \(\sigma(f') = f|_{D(s)}\) is
3116	  invertible, that is, \(j\bigl(D(f')\bigr) = D(f) \cap D(s)\).
3117	
3118	  Since \(j\) is an open immersion, applying the section functor to the identification of
3119	  the pullback with the restriction (\cref{lem:modules_restrictFunctorIsoPullback_mathlib})
3120	  gives additive isomorphisms
3121	  \(e_1 : \Gamma(M', \top) \cong \Gamma\bigl(M, D(s)\bigr)\) and
3122	  \(e_2 : \Gamma(M', D(f')) \cong \Gamma\bigl(M, D(f) \cap D(s)\bigr)\) which intertwine
3123	  the two restriction maps (naturality of the identification in the open) and are
3124	  \(\sigma\)-semilinear over the structure-sheaf comparisons: the action on the sections
3125	  of the pullback is through the immersion's section-ring isomorphism.
3126	
3127	  Finally apply the combiner \cref{lem:isLocalizedModule_powers_transport} with
3128	  \(A := \Gamma(\Spec R, D(s))\) — an \(R\)-algebra through restriction of global
3129	  sections — the isomorphism \(\sigma\), the elements \(f\) and \(f'\) (so that
3130	  \(\sigma(f')\) is the image of \(f\) in \(A\)), the localization
3131	  \(g := \bigl(\Gamma(M', \top) \to \Gamma(M', D(f'))\bigr)\), the comparisons
3132	  \(e_1, e_2\), and \(h := \bigl(\Gamma(M, D(s)) \to \Gamma(M, D(f) \cap D(s))\bigr)\):
3133	  the conclusion is that \(h\) is the localization at the powers of \(f\) over \(R\).
3134	\end{proof}
3135	
3136	\begin{lemma}[Section-localization descent for quasi-coherent modules (gap1 keystone)]
3137	  \label{lem:section_localization_descent}
3138	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_descent}
3139	  \leanok
3140	  \uses{lem:section_localization_descent_of_basicOpen_cover,
3141	    lem:section_localization_hfr_basicOpen}
3142	  For a quasi-coherent sheaf of modules \(M\) on \(\Spec R\) and \(f \in R\), the section
3143	  restriction \(\Gamma(M, \top) \to \Gamma(M, D(f))\) exhibits the target as the
3144	  localization \(\Gamma(M, \top)[1/f]\) over \(R\). This is the statement of
3145	  \cref{lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen} at the affine open \(\top\) of
3146	  \(\Spec R\), proved here by finite-cover descent --- not through the affine
3147	  equivalence of quasi-coherent modules with modules, which is instead \emph{derived}
3148	  from this lemma in \cref{lem:qcoh_affine_isIso_fromTildeΓ}.
3149	\end{lemma}
3150	\begin{proof}
3151	  \leanok
3152	  \uses{lem:section_localization_descent_of_basicOpen_cover,
3153	    lem:section_localization_hfr_basicOpen}
3154	  Quasi-coherence provides a cover \(\{X_i\}\) of \(\Spec R\) with per-member
3155	  presentations. Refine it to a finite basic-open cover: the basic opens form a basis of
3156	  \(\Spec R\), so every point has a basic-open neighbourhood contained in some \(X_i\);
3157	  quasi-compactness of \(\Spec R\) extracts a finite family \(t \subseteq R\) with each
3158	  \(D(r)\), \(r \in t\), contained in some \(X_i\), and covering is equivalent to \(t\)
3159	  generating the unit ideal. For every basic open \(D(u)\) contained in some \(D(r)\)
3160	  with \(r \in t\) — hence contained in a cover member \(X_i\) — the producer
3161	  \cref{lem:section_localization_hfr_basicOpen} supplies the localization datum at
3162	  \(D(u)\). The basic-open cover form
3163	  \cref{lem:section_localization_descent_of_basicOpen_cover} then yields the conclusion.
3164	\end{proof}
3165	
3166	
3167	\subsection{G1-assemble: from per-basic-open section localization to the
3168	\texorpdfstring{$\widetilde{(-)}$}{tilde}-counit}
3169	
3170	This subsection records the bridge \emph{section localization on every basic open}
3171	\(\Rightarrow\) \emph{invertible tilde--\(\Gamma\) counit}: given that the section
3172	restriction localizes on every basic open, the tilde--\(\Gamma\) counit is an
3173	isomorphism. Together with its converse
3174	\cref{lem:isLocalizedModule_restrict_of_isIso_fromTildeΓ} it assembles the equivalence
3175	\cref{lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_iff_isLocalizedModule_restrict}, which is exactly what makes G1-core
3176	(\cref{lem:qcoh_affine_section_localization}) and gap1
3177	(\cref{lem:qcoh_affine_isIso_fromTildeΓ}) interderivable -- the route by which G1-core is obtained as
3178	a corollary of gap1. The supporting Mathlib facts enter through the tilde--\(\Gamma\)
