3181	% --- Project-original G1-assemble nodes (Lean realizations) ---
3182	
3183	\begin{lemma}[Basic-open section localization for a module with invertible
3184	tilde--\(\Gamma\) counit]
3185	  \label{lem:isLocalizedModule_restrict_of_isIso_fromTildeΓ}
3186	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.isLocalizedModule_restrict_of_isIso_fromTildeΓ}
3187	  \leanok
3188	  \uses{lem:tilde_gamma_adjunction_mathlib}
3189	  % SOURCE: Stacks, Schemes, Lemma \texttt{lemma-widetilde-pullback} and the remark
3190	  % following it (restricting a tilde to a standard open D(f) yields the tilde of the
3191	  % localized module; read from references/stacks-schemes.tex, L1242-L1274).
3192	  \textit{Source: Stacks, Schemes, Lemma \texttt{widetilde-pullback} (sections of a
3193	  tilde over a basic open are the localized module).}
3194	  Let \(M\) be a sheaf of modules on \(\Spec R\) whose tilde--\(\Gamma\) counit
3195	  \(\widetilde{\Gamma(M, \top)} \to M\) (\cref{lem:tilde_gamma_adjunction_mathlib}) is an
3196	  isomorphism. Then for every \(f \in R\) the section restriction
3197	  \(\Gamma(M, \top) \to \Gamma(M, D(f))\) exhibits the target as the localization of
3198	  \(\Gamma(M, \top)\) at the powers of \(f\), over \(R\).
3199	\end{lemma}
3200	\begin{proof}
3201	  \leanok
3202	  \uses{lem:tilde_gamma_adjunction_mathlib}
3203	  First let \(M = \widetilde{N}\) be a tilde. The unit
3204	  \(N \to \Gamma(\widetilde{N}, \top)\) of the adjunction
3205	  (\cref{lem:tilde_gamma_adjunction_mathlib}) is an isomorphism, and the canonical map
3206	  \(N \to \Gamma(\widetilde{N}, D(f))\) exhibits the target as the localized module
3207	  \(N_f\) — the sections of a tilde over a basic open are the localization. The
3208	  canonical map factors as the unit followed by the section restriction, so the
3209	  restriction \(\Gamma(\widetilde{N}, \top) \to \Gamma(\widetilde{N}, D(f))\) is the
3210	  composite of the inverse unit isomorphism with a localization map, hence itself a
3211	  localization at the powers of \(f\).
3212	
3213	  For general \(M\), put \(N := \Gamma(M, \top)\). The counit isomorphism
3214	  \(\widetilde{N} \cong M\) induces, naturally in the open, \(R\)-linear isomorphisms
3215	  \(\Gamma(\widetilde{N}, \top) \cong \Gamma(M, \top)\) and
3216	  \(\Gamma(\widetilde{N}, D(f)) \cong \Gamma(M, D(f))\) intertwining the two restriction
3217	  maps. Pre- and post-composing a localization map with \(R\)-linear isomorphisms
3218	  compatible in this way preserves the localization property.
3219	\end{proof}
3220	
3221	\begin{lemma}[Characterization of the invertible tilde--\(\Gamma\) counit by
3222	section localization]
3223	  \label{lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_iff_isLocalizedModule_restrict}
3224	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.isIso_fromTildeΓ_iff_isLocalizedModule_restrict}
3225	  \leanok
3226	  \uses{lem:isLocalizedModule_restrict_of_isIso_fromTildeΓ,
3227	    lem:tilde_gamma_adjunction_mathlib}
3228	  For a sheaf of modules \(M\) on \(\Spec R\), the tilde--\(\Gamma\) counit
3229	  \(\widetilde{\Gamma(M, \top)} \to M\) is an isomorphism if and only if for every
3230	  \(f \in R\) the section restriction \(\Gamma(M, \top) \to \Gamma(M, D(f))\) exhibits
3231	  the target as the localization of \(\Gamma(M, \top)\) at the powers of \(f\) over
3232	  \(R\).
3233	\end{lemma}
3234	\begin{proof}
3235	  \leanok
3236	  \uses{lem:isLocalizedModule_restrict_of_isIso_fromTildeΓ,
3237	    lem:tilde_gamma_adjunction_mathlib}
3238	  The forward direction is \cref{lem:isLocalizedModule_restrict_of_isIso_fromTildeΓ}.
3239	
3240	  For the converse, write \(N := \Gamma(M, \top)\) and let
3241	  \(\varepsilon : \widetilde{N} \to M\) be the counit. Fix \(f \in R\). The
3242	  \(D(f)\)-component of \(\varepsilon\) is a map between two localizations of \(N\) at
3243	  the powers of \(f\): the source \(\Gamma(\widetilde{N}, D(f))\) receives the canonical
3244	  map from \(N\) (sections of a tilde over a basic open, as in the forward direction),
3245	  and the target \(\Gamma(M, D(f))\) receives the restriction
3246	  \(N = \Gamma(M, \top) \to \Gamma(M, D(f))\), a localization by hypothesis. The
3247	  component intertwines these two maps out of \(N\): the counit composed with the
3248	  canonical section map is the section restriction, by the compatibility of the counit
3249	  with the adjunction unit (\cref{lem:tilde_gamma_adjunction_mathlib}) and naturality.
3250	  A map between two localizations of the same module at the same multiplicative set
3251	  commuting with the two localization maps is the canonical isomorphism between them,
3252	  hence bijective. Thus \(\varepsilon\) is an isomorphism on every basic open.
3253	
3254	  Since the basic opens form a basis of \(\Spec R\), \(\varepsilon\) is an isomorphism
3255	  on every stalk: injective, because a germ in the kernel is represented by a section
3256	  over some basic neighbourhood killed by \(\varepsilon\) there, hence zero; surjective,
3257	  because every germ of \(M\) is the germ of a section over some basic open, which is
3258	  hit by the corresponding section of \(\widetilde{N}\). A morphism of sheaves that is
3259	  an isomorphism on all stalks is an isomorphism, and a morphism of sheaves of
3260	  modules whose underlying morphism of sheaves is an isomorphism is itself an
3261	  isomorphism.
3262	\end{proof}
3263	
3264	\begin{lemma}[A quasi-coherent module on an affine scheme is the tilde of its sections
3265	(gap1, Stacks 01I8)]

3273	  % isomorphic to the sheaf associated to the R-module of its global sections.
3274	  \textit{Source: Stacks~\href{https://stacks.math.columbia.edu/tag/01I8}{01I8}
3275	  (\texttt{lemma-quasi-coherent-affine}).}
3276	  For a quasi-coherent sheaf of modules \(M\) on \(\Spec R\), the tilde--\(\Gamma\)
3277	  counit \(\widetilde{\Gamma(M, \top)} \to M\) is an isomorphism; equivalently, \(M\)
3278	  lies in the essential image of the tilde functor, i.e. \(M\) is isomorphic to the
3279	  tilde of an \(R\)-module.
3280	\end{lemma}

3282	  \leanok

3284	    lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_iff_isLocalizedModule_restrict}

3286	  section restriction \(\Gamma(M, \top) \to \Gamma(M, D(f))\) is the localization at the

3288	  \cref{lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_iff_isLocalizedModule_restrict} concludes.
3289	\end{proof}

3304	\end{lemma}

3310	  \(M\) an isomorphism, and

3312	\end{proof}
